California Senior Living Facilities & Care Homes

16 senior living communities in California — pick a city on the map, or browse the list. Hover or tap a community to see where it is.

What's available in California

  • Assisted Living — 11 communities
  • Memory Care — 11 communities
  • Independent Living — 6 communities
  • Skilled Nursing — 6 communities
  • CCRC / Life Plan — 3 communities
  • Respite Care — 2 communities

How California regulates senior care

Assisted living and residential care homes are licensed as RCFEs by the Community Care Licensing Division; skilled nursing facilities are certified by CMS and licensed by the California Department of Public Health.

Small homes: Residential Care Facilities for the Elderly (RCFE) — small homes are licensed for up to 6 residents — how to evaluate one.

California resources for families

  • California Department of Social Services, Community Care Licensing Division — license & inspection lookup (how to read reports)
  • Eldercare Locator — 1-800-677-1116 — free federal referral line to local aging services
  • Your local Area Agency on Aging — options counseling and help paying for care
  • Long-Term Care Ombudsman — resident advocates who know each facility's complaint history

New to this?

Start with how to choose a facility, levels of care, and what it really costs.

Senior Living Community is an independent information resource for families. We are not a referral service and do not provide medical, legal, or financial advice — for decisions about care, consult the professionals involved. Facility details change; always confirm directly with a community. Last data review: 2026-07-11.
